titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Stanley B. Burns collection of postmortem and memorial photography and ephemera
Burns, Stanley B., collection of postmortem and memorial photography and ephemera
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 11, 2026 at 22:00

did.unittitle: Stanley B. Burns collection of postmortem and memorial photography and ephemera

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.physdesc: 28 boxes + 1 oversize folder + multiple volumes of reference materials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is unprocessed and materials are not listed, but access may be requested by contacting the Cushing/Whitney Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Stanley B. Burns, M.D., 2025, 2026.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Stanley B. Burns collection of postmortem and memorial photography and ephemera. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes approximately 1200 items collected by Stanley B. Burns, M.D., documenting funerals, mourning practices, cemetaries, and memorials in the United States and European countries. The collection includes photographs (the majority of material), a painting, metal coffin plates, a leather tombstone salesman case with stereoview cards and a viewer, memorial cards, other ephemera, and reference materials. Types of photographs include Daguerreotypes and other early cased photographs, cartes-de-visite, cabinet cards, stereoviews, albumin prints, gelatin silver prints, and others. Most of the photographs are black and white, many have been hand colored. The painting and several photographs are in original frames.

The collection was part of the holdings of the Burns Archive (burnsarchive.com) before being gifted to the Medical Historical Library. Many of the items in the collection have been used in Burns Archive publications and exhibitions, including the three volume Sleeping Beauty series.
Published works gifted with the collection will be cataloged individually.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
